body.title: Seed memory confidence from deposit-time source type
body.result: epistemic_source field added; SOURCE_MULTIPLIER in confidence property; stored in metadata, restored on load; 6 tests pass. Commit ea1e9aae.
body.intention: I intend that a memory's starting confidence reflects where it came from — experimentally verified knowledge starts higher than something read once in a book — so confidence evolves from a meaningful baseline rather than a flat type-based guess.
body.description:
Add `source: str = 'unknown'` field to the `Memory` dataclass with values `experimental | derived | read | observed | unknown`. Integrate into the `confidence` property as an initial seed multiplier applied to the BASE_CONFIDENCE baseline:

  SOURCE_MULTIPLIER = {
    'experimental': 1.0,   # you proved it yourself
    'derived':       0.85,  # you reasoned to it
    'read':          0.65,  # someone told you in a book
    'observed':      0.50,  # you saw it happen once
    'unknown':       0.75,  # default — no source info
  }

The source multiplier scales the BASE_CONFIDENCE value. System then evolves confidence from that starting point via existing activation_count / friction_history machinery.

Update `cortex.store()` to accept and persist `source` in memory metadata.

**Affected files:** devices/igor/memory/models.py, devices/igor/memory/cortex.py
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; postgres-only for persistence
**Scope boundary:** IN — source field, multiplier in confidence property, cortex.store() passthrough. OUT — changing existing deposit callers to pass source (they default to 'unknown' until each is updated).
**Completion criteria:** `Memory(narrative='x', memory_type=MemoryType.FACTUAL, source='experimental').confidence` > `Memory(narrative='x', memory_type=MemoryType.FACTUAL, source='read').confidence`; `cortex.store()` accepts source kwarg without error; 3 unit tests cover multiplier ordering.
